hero.description: Born Lê Anh Tú in 1981 in Hà Tĩnh, he left a career as a land surveyor in 2015 to practice Theravāda Dhutanga asceticism. After three silent pilgrimages, his 2024 walk across Vietnam became an unprecedented phenomenon — followed by millions.

biography.heading: From land surveyor to barefoot pilgrim
biography.intro: Lê Anh Tú was born in Hà Tĩnh in 1981, served in the military, and graduated from the Tây Nguyên College of Forestry. In 2015, he left his career and secular life to take the dharma name Minh Tuệ and begin practicing the strictest form of Theravāda asceticism.

t1.title: Born in Hà Tĩnh, raised in Gia Lai
t1.desc: Born 1981 in Kỳ Văn commune, Hà Tĩnh. Moved to Gia Lai in 1994. Completed military service and graduated from Tây Nguyên College of Forestry. Worked as a land surveyor.

t2.title: Renunciation & dharma name
t2.desc: In 2015, he left secular life and took the dharma name Minh Tuệ. He retreated to a mountain cave, begging daily for food and living by the strictest Dhutanga ascetic rules — no money, no possessions, no fixed abode.

t3.title: Three silent pilgrimages
t3.desc: From 2018 to 2023, he completed three walking pilgrimages across Vietnam in near-total obscurity, practicing Dhutanga with unwavering discipline and attracting almost no public attention.

t4.title: A nation watches — 2024
t4.desc: His fourth pilgrimage in 2024 became an unprecedented phenomenon. Thousands followed him on the road. Vietnamese authorities forcibly dispersed the crowd in June 2024, sparking international debate on religious freedom.

t5.title: International pilgrimage — Dec 2024
t5.desc: On December 12, 2024, he began an international pilgrimage walking through Laos, Thailand, Malaysia, Sri Lanka, India, and Nepal — retracing the path of the Buddha himself.

international.heading: The International Pilgrimage
international.description: On December 12, 2024, Minh Tuệ began walking beyond Vietnam's borders, tracing a path through six countries toward the birthplace of Buddhism. Accompanied only by a retired police officer who volunteered as his companion, he continues the same practice — no money, no possessions, walking on.

map.heading: 2,100 km on bare feet
map.description: Starting from Kiên Giang in the southwest, Minh Tuệ walked the length of Vietnam northward through more than 30 provinces to Hà Nội — a journey that captured the attention of millions.

info.title: About this archive
info.body: This is a non-profit documentary archive dedicated to preserving the spiritual journey of Minh Tuệ. We welcome contributions, corrections, and stories from the community.
